days.title: Arrival & the Marais
days.morning: Check in and walk the Marais neighbourhood. Visit Place des Vosges, the oldest square in Paris.
days.afternoon: Explore the Pompidou Centre exterior and nearby galleries. Coffee at Café de Flore.
days.evening: Dinner in the Marais — try L'As du Fallafel or a classic French bistro on Rue de Bretagne.
days.gem: The hidden courtyards (cours) of the Marais — most are open and completely tourist-free.

days.title: Louvre & Tuileries
days.morning: Louvre Museum — book timed entry online. Focus on one wing (Richelieu or Denon) to avoid overwhelm.
days.afternoon: Walk through Tuileries Garden to Place de la Concorde. Up the Champs-Élysées to the Arc de Triomphe.
days.evening: Drinks in the 8th arrondissement. Dinner near Saint-Lazare.
days.gem: The Louvre's medieval moat — visible from the basement level, almost no one goes.

days.title: Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
days.morning: Walk up to Sacré-Cœur early (before 9am) to avoid crowds. Explore the hilltop streets.
days.afternoon: Place du Tertre for artists, then down through Abbesses. Visit the Musée de Montmartre.
days.evening: Dinner in Pigalle — great natural wine bars on Rue des Martyrs.
days.gem: Vineyard of Montmartre — a real working vineyard inside Paris, hidden behind a stone wall.

days.title: Seine & Île de la Cité
days.morning: Notre-Dame exterior (reconstruction ongoing). Walk across Île Saint-Louis for the best ice cream in Paris (Berthillon).
days.afternoon: Sainte-Chapelle — stunning Gothic stained glass, worth every minute in the queue. Latin Quarter stroll.
days.evening: Dinner in Saint-Germain-des-Prés. Theatre or jazz if available.
days.gem: Square du Vert-Galant — a hidden garden at the tip of the island, locals come to read and picnic.

days.title: Musée d'Orsay & Saint-Germain
days.morning: Musée d'Orsay — Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces. Pre-book entry.
days.afternoon: Walk Saint-Germain: browse Librairie La Hune, visit the Luxembourg Gardens.
days.evening: Apéro at a café-terrasse. Dinner in the 6th.
days.gem: Top floor of Musée d'Orsay has a giant clock window overlooking the city — most visitors miss it.

days.title: Versailles Day Trip
days.morning: Take RER C to Versailles (40 min). Arrive early — Palace opens at 9am.
days.afternoon: Walk the Grand Canal and gardens. Return to Paris by 5pm.
days.evening: Easy dinner near your hotel — you'll be tired!
days.gem: The Trianon Palaces (Petit and Grand Trianon) — included in the ticket, much less crowded than the main palace.

days.title: Eiffel Tower & Farewell
days.morning: Eiffel Tower — summit ticket, book 2 weeks ahead. Visit in the morning for clear skies.
days.afternoon: Trocadéro for photos, then Palais de Tokyo if time allows. Last shopping on Rue de Rivoli.
days.evening: Farewell dinner — splurge on a brasserie classic: steak-frites and a carafe of Bordeaux.
days.gem: The view from Champ de Mars grass at dusk — free, relaxed, and somehow still magical.
